text: MarketReel — Global Film DistributionFord v FerrariFilm Synopsis & Narrative AnalysisMarketReel Intelligence Document 2019 Prepared for Acquisition ReviewProduction OverviewTitleFord v FerrariYear2019DirectorJames MangoldGenreDrama / Sports / BiographyRuntime152 minStudio / Distributor20th Century Fox / Chernin EntertainmentPrincipal CastMatt Damon, Christian Bale, Jon Bernthal, Caitriona Balfe, Tracy LettsExecutive SynopsisThe true story of visionary car designer Carroll Shelby and fearless British driver Ken Miles, who together built a revolutionary race car to challenge Ferrari's dominance at Le Mans 1966.Detailed Narrative SynopsisFord v Ferrari dramatizes one of motorsport's most legendary chapters: Ford Motor Company's obsessive campaign to defeat Ferrari at the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 1966. The film centers on two outsiders — pragmatic American car designer Carroll Shelby and hot-headed British racing driver Ken Miles — whose partnership produces the Ford GT40, a revolutionary machine that redefines what American engineering can achieve.The story begins in 1963 when Henry Ford II, stung by a failed attempt to purchase Ferrari, tasks his executives with building a race car that can beat Ferrari at Le Mans — the most prestigious endurance race in the world. After several failed attempts with committee-built cars, an exasperated Shelby is given limited creative freedom. He immediately turns to his friend Ken Miles, a brilliant but commercially unmarketable driver whose blunt honesty and mechanical genius are precisely what the project needs.The film's emotional core is the tension between the creative partnership of Shelby and Miles versus the institutional forces of Ford's corporate bureaucracy, personified by marketing executive Leo Beebe, who views Miles as a PR liability. Their 1965 Le Mans attempt ends in disaster. Returning in 1966, Shelby secures Miles the race he needs — but at the last moment, Ford executives order a controversial "photo finish" that denies Miles the historic clean-sweep victory he had earned, for marketing optics.The ending carries devastating weight: Miles, accepting the unjust outcome with quiet dignity, is killed weeks later testing a new prototype. Shelby visits the testing track alone, remembering his friend. The film closes with a quiet tribute to Miles — a man who gave everything to a vision that was ultimately taken from him.Narrative Themes for Distribution AnalysisIndividual genius vs. institutional conformityFriendship and loyaltyThe pursuit of perfectionCorporate corruption of pure achievementAmerican ambition and European craftsmanshipTone & Genre ClassificationGenre Classification: Drama / Sports / BiographyAudience Rating Profile: See Censorship & Certification Document for territory-specific ratings.Narrative Complexity: This document should be read in conjunction with the Cultural Sensitivity Report and Marketing Strategy Brief for complete acquisition analysis.MarketReel Confidential — Ford v Ferrari Synopsis Document — For Internal Distribution Use Only
